mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations, directly compressing forward S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in >65% of months where FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly increase in the unemployment rate signals immediate labor market deterioration, prompting market participants to price in recessionary monetary easing, generating positive 5-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >70% of instances following a >0.2pp UNRATE jump, or signal loses predictive power when UNRATE exceeds 5.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a synchronized collapse in consumer demand, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint declines in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict negative 60-day returns with a Rank IC > 0.04, or the interaction term loses significance in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: An expansion in the index of industrial production signals strengthening economic output, which supports equity valuations and produces positive 20-day S&P 500 returns when inflation is below 5%.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict positive 20-day returns when CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth exceeds 5.0%, or the signal reverses direction in high-inflation regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained consumer price index levels above 295 indicate entrenched inflation that forces central banks to maintain restrictive policy, suppressing equity valuations and generating negative 60-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in >60% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds 295, or the threshold effect vanishes when FEDFUNDS rises above 4.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects robust labor market expansion, which sustains consumer spending and corporate profitability, driving positive 20-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth fails to predict positive 20-day returns with a Rank IC > 0.03 over rolling 3-year windows, or the signal breaks down during supply-constrained periods.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between quarterly GDP growth and monthly retail sales, where retail sales outpace GDP trends, signals accelerating near-term demand that boosts equity momentum and yields positive 5-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: The RSAFS-GDPC1 divergence fails to predict positive 5-day returns with statistical significance in out-of-sample testing, or the signal reverses when HOUST declines.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive monthly declines in industrial production signal a manufacturing contraction that reduces corporate revenue expectations, triggering negative 20-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Consecutive INDPRO declines fail to predict negative 20-day returns in >55% of historical instances, or predictive accuracy drops below random chance during financial stress periods.
